meta.title: Hydrogenophilus thermoluteolus gen. nov., sp. nov., a thermophilic, facultatively chemolithoautotrophic, hydrogen-oxidizing bacterium
meta.journalTitle: International Journal of Systematic and Evolutionary Microbiology

abstractHtml: The taxonomic positions of 'Pseudomonas hydrogenothermophila' strain TH-1 and 'Flavobacterium autothermophilum' strain TH-4 were studied by 16S rDNA sequencing. These organisms are Gram-negative, strictly aerobic, thermophilic, facultatively chemolithoautotrophic hydrogen-oxidizing rods and have a DNA G+C content of 63--65 mol%. The major isoprenoid quinone is ubiquinone-8 and 3-hydroxy decanoic acid (3-OH C(10:0)) is the major 3-hydroxy cellular fatty acid. A phylogenetic analysis based on 16S rDNA sequences placed strains TH-1(T) and TH-4 in the beta-subclass of the Proteobacteria. The taxonomic characteristics of these organisms are different from those of previously described aerobic, facultatively chemolithoautotrophic, hydrogen-oxidizing bacteria that belong to the beta-subclass of Proteobacteria. On the basis of the information described above, a new genus and species, Hydrogenophilus thermoluteolus gen. nov., sp. nov., is described to include both strains. The type strain is strain TH-1(T) (=IFO 14978(T)).
